---
title: "Research"
output: html_document
---

```{=html}
<style>
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cormorant+Garamond:wght@500;600;700&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap');

body {
  background: #fafafa;
}

.research-page {
  max-width: 1080px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 56px 24px 72px;
  font-family: "Inter", sans-serif;
  color: #1f2933;
}

.research-hero {
  margin-bottom: 42px;
  padding: 36px 40px;
  border-radius: 30px;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f7f1e8 0%, #eef4ff 50%, #ffffff 100%);
  border: 1px solid rgba(30, 64, 175, 0.12);
  box-shadow: 0 18px 45px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.08);
}

.research-kicker {
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.16em;
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #7c5c2e;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.research-title {
  font-family: "Cormorant Garamond", serif;
  font-size: 3.5rem;
  line-height: 1.05;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin: 0 0 14px;
  color: #111827;
}

.research-summary {
  max-width: 850px;
  font-size: 1.5rem;
  line-height: 1.75;
  color: #4b5563;
  margin: 0;
}

.research-focus {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 10px;
  margin-top: 22px;
}

.focus-tag {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 8px 13px;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
  border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);
  color: #475569;
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.section-label {
  font-family: "Cormorant Garamond", serif;
  font-size: 2.35rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #111827;
  margin: 42px 0 20px;
}

.featured-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: 24px;
}

.research-card {
  position: relative;
  padding: 30px 32px;
  border-radius: 26px;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.96);
  border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.28);
  box-shadow: 0 14px 35px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.07);
  overflow: hidden;
}

.research-card::before {
  content: "";
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  top: 0;
  width: 8px;
  height: 100%;
  background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #1d4ed8, #0f766e);
}

.research-card.gold::before {
  background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #b08968, #7c5c2e);
}

.research-card.teal::before {
  background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #0f766e, #14b8a6);
}

.research-card.blue::before {
  background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #1d4ed8, #60a5fa);
}

.research-meta {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 10px;
  margin-bottom: 14px;
}

.meta-pill {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 7px 12px;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: #f3f4f6;
  color: #6b7280;
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.project-title {
  font-family: "Cormorant Garamond", serif;
  font-size: 2.05rem;
  line-height: 1.15;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #111827;
  margin: 0 0 10px;
}

.project-role {
  font-size: 1.25rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #374151;
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

.project-description {
  font-size: 1.3rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
  color: #4b5563;
  margin: 0 0 16px;
}

.project-list {
  margin: 0;
  padding-left: 1.25rem;
  color: #4b5563;
}

.project-list li {
  font-size: 1.22rem;
  line-height: 1.65;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.project-tags {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 8px;
  margin-top: 18px;
}

.project-tag {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 7px 11px;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: #f8fafc;
  border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);
  color: #475569;
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 600;
}

.research-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
  gap: 24px;
}

.compact-card {
  padding: 26px 28px;
  border-radius: 24px;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.96);
  border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.28);
  box-shadow: 0 14px 35px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.06);
}

.compact-title {
  font-family: "Cormorant Garamond", serif;
  font-size: 1.85rem;
  line-height: 1.15;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #111827;
  margin: 0 0 10px;
}

.compact-meta {
  font-size: 1.1rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #6b7280;
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

.compact-description {
  font-size: 1.18rem;
  line-height: 1.68;
  color: #4b5563;
  margin: 0;
}

@media (max-width: 820px) {
  .research-page {
    padding: 36px 16px 56px;
  }

  .research-hero {
    padding: 28px 24px;
  }

  .research-title {
    font-size: 2.65rem;
  }

  .research-summary {
    font-size: 1.25rem;
  }

  .research-grid {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }

  .project-title {
    font-size: 1.85rem;
  }

  .project-description,
  .project-list li {
    font-size: 1.12rem;
  }
}
</style>

<main class="research-page">

<section class="research-hero">
  <div class="research-kicker">Research Portfolio</div>
  <h1 class="research-title">Research</h1>
  <p class="research-summary">
    My research focuses on computational biomedical science, with an emphasis on pharmacogenomics,
    adverse drug reaction prediction, single-cell omics, multi-omics integration, and statistical learning
    for genomics and precision medicine.
  </p>

  <div class="research-focus">
    <span class="focus-tag">Pharmacogenomics</span>
    <span class="focus-tag">Single-cell Omics</span>
    <span class="focus-tag">Multi-omics Integration</span>
    <span class="focus-tag">Hypergraph Learning</span>
    <span class="focus-tag">Biomedical Informatics</span>
  </div>
</section>

<h2 class="section-label">Current Research</h2>

<section class="featured-grid">

  <article class="research-card blue">
    <div class="research-meta">
      <span class="meta-pill">Research Collaborator</span>
      <span class="meta-pill">Jul. 2025 – Current</span>
      <span class="meta-pill">Supervisor: Dr. Jun Wen</span>
    </div>

    <h2 class="project-title">HyperADRs: Hierarchical Hypergraph Learning for Drug–Gene–ADR Prediction</h2>

    <div class="project-role">
      Pharmacogenomics · Hypergraph Neural Networks · Adverse Drug Reactions
    </div>

    <p class="project-description">
      This project develops a hierarchical hypergraph framework for predicting adverse drug reactions by modeling
      drug–gene–ADR relationships as biologically interpretable hyperedges. The work integrates pharmacogenomic
      knowledge, causal gene and target information, and pathway-level evidence to improve ADR prediction.
    </p>

    <ul class="project-list">
      <li>Constructed relational hypergraphs over drug–gene–ADR triplets for query-conditioned ADR prediction.</li>
      <li>Designed ADR categorization strategies to reduce label sparsity and improve physiologically coherent prediction.</li>
      <li>Benchmarked hypergraph learning models against MLP, GCN, GAT, and HGNN baselines under inductive splits.</li>
      <li>Coordinated manuscript writing, figure preparation, methods description, and reproducibility materials.</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="project-tags">
      <span class="project-tag">HyperADRs</span>
      <span class="project-tag">HGCN</span>
      <span class="project-tag">PharmGKB</span>
      <span class="project-tag">DrugBank</span>
      <span class="project-tag">ADR Prediction</span>
    </div>
  </article>

  <article class="research-card teal">
    <div class="research-meta">
      <span class="meta-pill">Research Collaborator</span>
      <span class="meta-pill">Oct. 2025 – Current</span>
      <span class="meta-pill">Supervisor: Dr. Jun Wen</span>
    </div>

    <h2 class="project-title">Variant-Level Tensor Modeling for Drug–Disease–ADR Relationships</h2>

    <div class="project-role">
      Tensor Decomposition · Variant-Level Genomics · Pharmacogenetics
    </div>

    <p class="project-description">
      This project investigates variant-level genetic effects on drug–disease–ADR relationships using directional
      co-occurrence matrices and tensor-based modeling. The goal is to quantify how curated pharmacogenomic variants
      deviate from baseline variant patterns.
    </p>

    <ul class="project-list">
      <li>Integrated PharmGKB toxicity variants with large-scale baseline variant sets for comparative modeling.</li>
      <li>Constructed variant-indexed directed co-occurrence matrices representing prior and posterior dependencies.</li>
      <li>Implemented Python pipelines for sparse matrix parsing, cohort-size normalization, and integrity checking.</li>
      <li>Planned tensor decomposition analyses using Tucker and CP models to evaluate variant-level deviation signals.</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="project-tags">
      <span class="project-tag">Tensor Decomposition</span>
      <span class="project-tag">PharmGKB</span>
      <span class="project-tag">Sparse Matrices</span>
      <span class="project-tag">Variant Modeling</span>
    </div>
  </article>

</section>

<h2 class="section-label">Selected Previous Research</h2>

<section class="research-grid">

  <article class="compact-card">
    <h3 class="compact-title">Multi-omics Integration for Disease Subtyping</h3>
    <div class="compact-meta">Columbia University · Supervisor: Dr. Shuang Wang · Apr. 2024 – May 2025</div>
    <p class="compact-description">
      Conducted literature review and simulation studies on advanced multi-omics integration methods for disease
      subtyping, with emphasis on clustering algorithms, parameter tuning, and comparative evaluation.
    </p>
  </article>

  <article class="compact-card">
    <h3 class="compact-title">All of Us EHR Analysis for Breast and Prostate Cancer</h3>
    <div class="compact-meta">Columbia University · Supervisor: Dr. Tian Gu · Sep. 2024 – May 2025</div>
    <p class="compact-description">
      Explored electronic health record data from the All of Us database and applied angle-based transfer learning
      methods to analyze breast and prostate cancer-related data.
    </p>
  </article>

  <article class="compact-card">
    <h3 class="compact-title">Vision-Related Quality-of-Life in NYC-SIGHT</h3>
    <div class="compact-meta">Columbia University · Supervisors: Dr. Prakash Gorroochurn and Dr. Lisa Hark · Feb. 2024 – Aug. 2024</div>
    <p class="compact-description">
      Performed statistical analyses of NEI-VFQ-9 outcomes in a randomized clinical trial, including group comparisons
      and multivariable regression to identify predictors of improvement in vision-related quality-of-life.
    </p>
  </article>

  <article class="compact-card">
    <h3 class="compact-title">Clinical Characteristics in AMD and Diabetic Retinopathy</h3>
    <div class="compact-meta">Columbia University · Supervisor: Dr. Alan Morse · Apr. 2024 – Aug. 2024</div>
    <p class="compact-description">
      Compared demographic and clinical characteristics in patients with age-related macular degeneration and diabetic
      retinopathy, contributing to published analyses of disease-related behavioral and clinical factors.
    </p>
  </article>

  <article class="compact-card">
    <h3 class="compact-title">CYP450 and sEH Pathways in Alzheimer’s Disease</h3>
    <div class="compact-meta">University of Toronto · Supervisor: Dr. Walter Swardfager · Sep. 2021 – Apr. 2022</div>
    <p class="compact-description">
      Investigated lipid-metabolism-related genetic pathways in Alzheimer’s disease using candidate SNPs and
      multidimensional analysis of ADNI data.
    </p>
  </article>

  <article class="compact-card">
    <h3 class="compact-title">Duckweed–Microbiome Interactions</h3>
    <div class="compact-meta">University of Toronto · Supervisor: Dr. Megan Frederickson · Sep. 2021 – Apr. 2022</div>
    <p class="compact-description">
      Studied species-specific duckweed–microbiome interactions using visualization and image analysis approaches.
    </p>
  </article>

</section>

</main>
```
